cot_prefix: Okay, so the question is about whether a man can legally leave all his money to his widow's sister, who he plans to marry next year. Let me break this down step by step. First, I need to understand the sequence of events. The man has passed away, and now his estate is being distributed. He left all his money to his widow's sister. But the twist is that he was planning to marry the sister of his widow next year. So, the sister of the widow was the intended new spouse, but the man has already died. So the key issue here is whether the man can legally designate his future spouse (who is currently the sister of his deceased wife) as the beneficiary of his estate.
